meta.name: notion
meta.description: Notion marketing site (notion.com) — warm-white AI-workspace landing: bold Inter-class headlines, hand-drawn ink illustration, single vivid blue CTA, product UI as imagery.

color.palette_type: monochromatic neutrals + single blue accent, with a full multi-hue primitive ramp reserved for illustration and badges

primary.role: CTA buttons, links, highlight pill fills (blue-500; hover lightens to #62aef0)

secondary.role: headline text, dark sections, logo ink (gray-900)

accent.role: highlight pill/chip backgrounds behind key words (blue-200)

neutral.usage: warm-biased grays: #fff page ground, gray-100/200 section tints and cards, gray-500 muted text, gray-900 ink; alpha-black/white ramps (5%–95%) for borders and overlays

color.contrast_strategy: high contrast dark-on-light; near-black ink on white with only the blue accent saturated; dark inverted sections use alpha-white text ramps

font_families.heading: NotionInter, Inter, -apple-system, sans-serif
font_families.mono: iA Writer Mono, Nitti, Menlo, monospace

typography.font_style_notes: One grotesque family (custom Inter build) carries everything; personality comes from weight contrast — heavy 700 headlines against regular body. Lyon Text serif and Permanent Marker handwriting exist as product/editorial voices, not marketing chrome. Token ramp: font-size-50 (0.75rem) → font-size-1100 (6rem).

spacing.content_density: comfortable — roomy sections, but feature grids pack tighter than the hero
spacing.section_rhythm: large uniform vertical bands; alternating white and gray-100/gray-200 tinted sections; occasional dark inverted band

layout.grid_system: centered container with CSS-grid feature card rows
layout.max_content_width: ~1200px marketing container; hero text narrower (~900px) and centered
layout.columns: 12-col conceptual; card rows render as 2–4 columns
layout.alignment_tendency: centered hero and section intros; left-aligned inside cards

shape.border_usage: subtle 1px alpha-black borders on cards and inputs; bold 2–3px colored circle strokes around illustration avatars
shape.divider_style: hairline alpha-black-200; sections mostly separated by background-tint changes instead of rules

levels.high: 3-layer stack up to ~18px blur, ≤2% alpha per layer (shadow-300)

elevation.depth_cues: background-tint changes and borders first; shadows only float product-UI windows and dropdowns

iconography.style: outlined UI icons plus hand-drawn ink spot illustrations
iconography.stroke_weight: 1.5px UI icons; variable brush weight in illustrations
iconography.preferred_set: Notion's in-house icon set; do not substitute a generic library when reproducing

motion.easing: ease-out for fades in, ease-in for fades out, cubic-bezier(0.76,0,0.24,1)/quint for transforms (motion-timing-function tokens)
motion.entrance_pattern: fade-in + slide-down for nav/menus; staggered enter animations in agent demo panels
motion.philosophy: minimal functional chrome motion, plus choreographed self-playing product demos (agent tasks, notification counters, logo marquee)

components.button_style: solid blue-500 pill-or-8px-radius primary with white text (hover blue-400); white secondary with blue text; ghost buttons on dark sections use alpha-white fills
components.card_style: gray-100/200 tinted cards, 12–16px radius, 1px alpha border, hairline shadow, screenshot or illustration up top
components.navigation_pattern: sticky top nav with mega-menu dropdowns; nav background fades in on scroll with a shadow keyframe
components.modal_style: white sheet, 12px radius, shadow-300, dimmed alpha-black overlay
components.list_style: arrow-suffixed link lists ('Docs → ', 'Projects → '); checklists inside product mock UI
components.component_notes: Badges come in bold/light/muted/outline variants driven by --color-accent-strong, which is re-themed per campaign section (blue, orange, purple, teal…). Product-UI windows with traffic lights serve as the imagery.

aesthetic.visual_metaphor: paper document workspace — white sheet, ink drawings, blue pen highlights
aesthetic.era_influence: 2020s SaaS minimalism crossed with mid-century New Yorker cartoon linework

visual_language.complexity: moderate — simple chrome, dense self-playing product demos
visual_language.ornamentation: subtle accents: highlight pills behind key words, colored circle strokes, hand-drawn faces
visual_language.whitespace_usage: generous around headlines; section tints keep whitespace from feeling empty
visual_language.visual_weight_distribution: heavy centered headline blocks, weight balanced by illustration clusters below
visual_language.focal_strategy: single hero statement, then distributed interest across card grids
visual_language.texture_usage: ink-brush texture inside illustrations only; UI surfaces are flat

composition.hierarchy_method: scale + weight contrast; one accent color marks the action
composition.balance_type: symmetric hero, mosaic card rows below
composition.flow_direction: vertical scroll with alternating tinted bands
composition.grouping_strategy: cards group features; arrow links chain related items
composition.negative_space_role: frames the oversized headline; gives illustrations air

imagery.photo_treatment: customer-story photos only, small and card-framed; no hero photography
imagery.illustration_style: two related sets: (1) small hand-drawn ink spot icons of anthropomorphized objects with dotted eyes — alarm clock, light bulb, signpost, speech bubble, globe, puzzle piece — on transparent grounds (~80-100px PNGs); (2) hero medallions: perfect circles holding black-ink faces and objects with halftone/screentone shading, ring strokes or solid fills in flat blue #62aef0-ish, red #f64932-ish, and yellow — New Yorker-cartoon linework meets flat spot color
imagery.graphic_elements: highlight pills behind words, blue dot bullets, arrow glyphs, avatar medallions, logo marquee
imagery.pattern_usage: none — flat grounds and tinted bands instead of patterns
imagery.image_shape: rounded-rectangle product windows (12–16px) and perfect-circle illustration medallions

interaction_feel.feedback_style: quiet — color shifts and small fades, no bouncy physics
interaction_feel.hover_behavior: buttons lighten one ramp step; links gain underline or arrow shift
interaction_feel.microinteraction_density: low in chrome, high inside the scripted product demos

brand_voice_in_ui.tone: plainspoken and benefit-first with a wink ('Perfectly written by AI', 'Less tracking. More progress.')
brand_voice_in_ui.cta_style: direct imperative: 'Get Notion free', 'Request a demo'

overview.fallback_strategy: effects are plain CSS keyframes; reduced-motion users get static frames

background_effects.description: flat white and gray-tint section bands; dark inverted band for contrast

scroll_triggered_animations.trigger_points: nav on first scroll; demo panels on viewport entry
scroll_triggered_animations.animation_type: fade-up entrances; nav background/shadow fade-in; scripted agent-task sequences (Agent_*Enter/Scroll keyframes); animated notification counters

text_effects.description: headline emphasis is static — blue-200 pill highlight behind a key word with a blue dot, echoing an inline mention chip

image_effects.description: product screenshots sit static in rounded windows; customer video behind a play button

svg_animations.description: SVGs are static logos, icons, and ink illustrations

visual_effects.composite_notes: The site's liveliness comes from scripted, looping product demos built in DOM/CSS: agent task lists that type and check themselves off (Agent_* keyframes), rolling notification counters, and a customer-logo marquee (Marquee_marqueeFrames). Campaign sub-sections re-theme the token layer (--color-accent-strong swaps to orange/purple/teal, and a dev-platform section goes full DOS-terminal with neon-on-dark). Everything degrades gracefully — no canvas, WebGL, or animation libraries detected in the homepage payload.
